signals.reasoning: BTC is stable and liquid, but the directional edge is still weak and the board keeps it in no-trade territory. Slightly negative funding helps, but not enough to justify fresh exposure in a RED macro regime.
ETH remains soft and relatively low-energy, with positive funding and no-trade status on the board. It is neither a compelling long nor a compelling short here.
SOL has modest constructive price action, but funding is still positive and the board still says no-trade. Not enough asymmetry to deploy capital.
ZEC has real liquidity and one of the better board profiles outside the squeeze names, but the move is already extended and cascade risk is elevated. Interesting watchlist long, not a priority entry.
MERL has mild upside momentum, but liquidity is thin, spread is poor, and the mechanical edge is still neutral. Easy pass.

TURBO still has a legitimate squeeze-continuation setup with strong momentum, deeply negative funding, and high squeeze plus crowding scores. The main limitation is execution quality and macro still being RED, so this is reduced-size only.
GRIFFAIN has some follow-through, but the board is mixed and spread quality is mediocre. It is not clean enough to rank with the top long candidates.
VVV has solid momentum and decent liquidity, but funding is positive and the mechanical board is still neutral. It is interesting, but not stronger than TNSR, BLUR, or TURBO.
COMP is constructive but still not clean. Positive funding, middling liquidity, and a neutral board keep it below trade threshold.

TNSR remains one of the strongest squeeze-continuation names on the sheet with big 24h expansion, negative funding, and a clear bullish trade flag. Macro still being RED keeps confidence capped, but this is still a real reduced-size long.

BLUR still offers a strong continuation-long profile with good volume, negative funding, and a bullish mechanical board. Its spread is a little better than some peers, but macro risk still keeps this from becoming a high-conviction all-in signal.
